cases.title: [My Account] Verify that the My Account page renders with all required fields and controls
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page (reached via the Profile icon menu in the top right of the Dashboard, which lists Organisations & Sites, Team, Billing & Invoices, MCP access, Notifications, My account, and Logout — this is a dedicated Settings page with its own left-hand navigation, not a modal). The registered email address has not yet been verified.

steps.content: Observe the "Account details" section at the top of the page.
steps.expected: An "Account details" heading should be displayed with the registered email shown (read-only) in a disabled text field, and a "Change email" button on the right of the field.

steps.content: Observe the "Email notifications" section below Account details.
steps.expected: An "Email notifications" heading should be displayed with the text "Choose the type of emails you'd like to receive from us." and a "Manage email preferences" button. [fixed: new section found live, not present in Suite 6 source]

steps.content: Observe the "Security" section below Email notifications.
steps.expected: A "Security" heading should be displayed with a "Change password" button and a "Set up 2FA" button (labelled "Two-factor authentication (2FA)").

steps.content: Hover over the "Set up 2FA" button.
steps.expected: The button should be disabled, and a tooltip "Please verify your email address to set up 2FA." should be displayed. [needs-manual-check: could not reproduce with the seeded QA2 test account, whose email is already verified — re-verify with a fresh/unverified signup]

steps.content: Observe the "Account deletion" section below the Security section.
steps.expected: An "Account deletion" heading should be displayed with the text "Once you delete your account, all your data will be lost forever" and a "Delete account" button.

cases.rewrite_notes: Stripped role/plan prefixes; collapsed navigation steps into Preconditions; dropped pure alignment checks.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that the My Account page shows Disable 2FA and Re-generate recovery codes controls when 2FA is already enabled
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. Two-factor authentication (2FA) has already been set up on the account.

steps.content: Observe the Security section of the My Account page.
steps.expected: The "Set up 2FA" button should no longer be displayed. "Disable 2FA" and "Re-generate recovery codes" buttons should be displayed in its place, the latter under a "Re-generate your 2FA recovery codes" label. [confirmed: verified live by completing the 2FA setup flow]

steps.content: Observe the "Account details" and "Account deletion" sections.
steps.expected: Both should remain unchanged from the default state. [confirmed]

cases.rewrite_notes: Entire source group was an empty stub across all 14 duplicates in Suite 6. Content inferred from the button states referenced in the 2FA setup/disable/re-generate flows; the inference was then confirmed live.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that updating the registered email requires verifying a code sent to the new address before the change takes effect
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page.

steps.content: Click the edit icon next to the registered email field.
steps.expected: The "Update your email address" modal should be displayed with a "New email address" field, "Confirm email address" field, "Current password" field, an alert stating "A verification code will be sent to the new email address," and "Cancel" / "Update email" buttons.

steps.content: Enter a valid new email address in "New email address," the same address in "Confirm email address," and the account password in "Current password."
steps.expected: The values should be accepted in all three fields.

steps.content: Click "Update email."
steps.expected: The modal should close and an "Enter verification code" pop-up should be displayed with six digit fields, a "resend code" link, and "Cancel" / "Confirm Code" buttons (the latter disabled by default).

steps.content: Check the new email address inbox.
steps.expected: A "Verify your new email for CookieYes" email should be received, containing a 6-digit code that expires in 10 minutes.

steps.content: Enter the 6-digit code from the email and click "Confirm Code."
steps.expected: A "Your email has been changed!" pop-up should be displayed, stating the user will be logged out and must log back in with the new email address.

steps.content: Click "Okay" on the pop-up.
steps.expected: The user should be logged out and the Log In page should be displayed.

steps.content: Log in with the new email address and the account password.
steps.expected: The Dashboard should be displayed, and the My Account page should show the new email as the registered email.

cases.rewrite_notes: Merged 7 fragmented Suite 6 cases (edit icon, modal display, valid-fields submit, code pop-up display, verification email display, confirm-code valid, email-changed pop-up) into one end-to-end flow.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that updating the email with empty fields shows validation errors
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Update your email address" modal is open.

steps.content: Leave "New email address," "Confirm email address," and "Current password" empty, and click "Update email."
steps.expected: Error messages should be displayed under each field: "New email is required," "Confirm Email is required," and "Current Password is required."

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that entering mismatched new and confirm email addresses shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Update your email address" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter a valid email address in "New email address," a different valid email address in "Confirm email address," and a valid password in "Current password."
steps.expected: The values should be accepted in all fields.

steps.content: Click "Update email."
steps.expected: "New email and Confirm email does not match" error message should be displayed under the "Confirm email address" field.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that entering an invalid new email address shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Update your email address" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter an invalid email address (e.g. missing "@") in "New email address."
steps.expected: The value should be accepted in the field.

steps.content: Click "Update email."
steps.expected: "Valid email required" error message should be displayed under the "New email address" field. [fixed: live app shows "Valid email required" for an invalid-format entry, not "New email is required" — that message only appears when the field is empty]

cases.rewrite_notes: Carried over the source's expected text verbatim even though it reads oddly for an invalid-format input (says "required" rather than "invalid format") — not altering per the no-assumptions rule; flag for /grill-section to verify against the live app.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that entering an incorrect current password shows a validation error when updating email
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Update your email address" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter a valid new email address in "New email address," the same address in "Confirm email address," and an incorrect password in "Current password."
steps.expected: The values should be accepted in all fields.

steps.content: Click "Update email."
steps.expected: "Invalid password. Please try again" error message should be displayed as a general form message above the Cancel/Update email buttons. [fixed: live app renders this as a form-level message, not anchored under the Current password field]

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that cancelling the verification code entry closes the pop-up without updating the email
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Enter verification code" pop-up is open after submitting a valid email change request.

steps.content: Click "Cancel" on the "Enter verification code" pop-up.
steps.expected: The pop-up should close and the registered email should remain unchanged. [inferred: source only asserted the pop-up closes]

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that entering an invalid verification code shows an error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Enter verification code" pop-up is open.

steps.content: Enter an invalid 6-digit code in the digit fields.
steps.expected: The value should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Confirm Code."
steps.expected: "Invalid verification code. Please check your email for the correct code." error message should be displayed.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that an expired verification code shows an error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Enter verification code" pop-up is open, and more than 10 minutes have passed since the code was sent.

steps.content: Enter the (now expired) 6-digit code from the email.
steps.expected: The value should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Confirm Code."
steps.expected: "The verification code has expired. Please resend code and try again." error message should be displayed.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that resending the verification code restarts the resend cooldown
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Enter verification code" pop-up is open.

steps.content: Click the "Resend Code" button.
steps.expected: A new "Verify your new email for CookieYes" email should be sent, and the button should be replaced by a countdown showing "Resend code in [seconds] seconds." [fixed: live element is a button labelled "Resend Code", not a lowercase "resend code" link]

steps.content: Wait for the countdown to finish.
steps.expected: The "Resend Code" button should be displayed again.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that changing the password with a valid old password and matching new password lets the user log in with the new password
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page.

steps.content: Click "Change password."
steps.expected: The "Change password" modal should be displayed with "Old password," "New password," and "Confirm password" fields, and "Cancel" / "Change Password" buttons.

steps.content: Enter the current password in "Old password," a new valid password in "New password," and the same value in "Confirm password."
steps.expected: The values should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Change password."
steps.expected: [needs-manual-check] Reproducible bug on QA2: the /settings/account page goes completely blank (no toaster, no visible content) immediately after a successful submit, before the app auto-logs-out and redirects to the Log In page. The password change itself DOES take effect (confirmed via re-login below) — only the immediate on-page feedback is broken. Draft's original expectation ("modal closes, toaster 'Your password has been changed.' shown") could not be observed live; flag for engineering review before finalizing this expected result.

steps.content: Log out and log back in using the new password.
steps.expected: The Dashboard should be displayed, confirming the new password is active.

cases.rewrite_notes: Merged modal-open + happy-path into one flow (collapses 3 Suite 6 groups); kept the re-login step as it's the direct oracle that the change took effect.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that cancelling the Change password modal discards entered values
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Change password" modal is open.

steps.content: Click "Cancel."
steps.expected: The "Change password" modal should close.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that submitting the Change password form with empty fields shows validation errors
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Change password" modal is open.

steps.content: Leave all three fields empty and click "Change Password."
steps.expected: Error messages should be displayed: "Old password is required," "New password is required," and "Confirm password is required" under the respective fields.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that an incorrect old password shows a validation error when changing password
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Change password" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter an incorrect password in "Old password," a valid new password in "New password," and the same value in "Confirm password."
steps.expected: The values should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Change password."
steps.expected: "Invalid password. Please try again" error message should be displayed as a general form message above the Cancel/Change password buttons. [fixed: submit button is labelled "Change password" (lowercase p), not "Change Password"; live app renders this error as a form-level message, not anchored under the Confirm password field]

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that a new password not meeting complexity requirements shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Change password" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter the correct old password, an invalid new password (not meeting complexity rules) in "New password," and the same value in "Confirm password."
steps.expected: The values should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Change Password."
steps.expected: "Include both lowercase and uppercase characters, at least one number, and a special character such as ! @ # $ % ^" error message should be displayed under "New password."

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that mismatched new password and confirm password shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Change password" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter the correct old password, a new password in "New password," and a different password in "Confirm password."
steps.expected: The values should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Change Password."
steps.expected: "New Password and Confirm Password does not match" error message should be displayed under "New password."

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that setting up 2FA with a valid authenticator code activates 2FA and displays recovery codes
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The registered email has been verified.

steps.content: Click "Set up 2FA."
steps.expected:
The "Set up two-factor authentication (2FA)" modal should be displayed with:
- A QR code to scan with an authenticator app
- A setup key with a copy icon, for manual entry
- A verification code field
- "Cancel" and "Verify Code" buttons

steps.content: Click the copy icon next to the setup key.
steps.expected: The setup key should be copied.

steps.content: Add the setup key to an authenticator app and enter the resulting verification code in the 6-digit field.
steps.expected: The value should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Verify code." [fixed: button is labelled "Verify code", lowercase c]
steps.expected:
A "2FA has been set up successfully on your account!" pop-up should be displayed with:
- A success icon
- An alert: "Make sure you copy and save your 2FA recovery codes. If you lose access to your device, these codes can be used to log in to your account. Each code can be used only once."
- A table of 12 ten-digit recovery codes with a "Copy" button
- An "I've securely saved my 2FA recovery codes." checkbox
- An "Okay" button, disabled by default

steps.content: Click the "Copy" button on the recovery code table.
steps.expected: The 12 recovery codes should be copied.

steps.content: Check "I’ve securely saved my 2FA recovery codes." and click "Okay."
steps.expected: The pop-up should close.

steps.content: Observe the My Account page.
steps.expected: "Disable 2FA" and "Re-generate recovery codes" buttons should now be displayed in place of "Set up 2FA."

cases.rewrite_notes: Consolidated 8 fragmented per-button Suite 6 cases into one end-to-end setup flow.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that entering an invalid verification code during 2FA setup shows an error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Set up two-factor authentication (2FA)" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter an invalid verification code in the 6-digit field.
steps.expected: The value should be accepted.

steps.content: Click "Verify code."
steps.expected: "Invalid authentication code" error message should be displayed under the 6-digit field. [fixed: live button is labelled "Verify code" (lowercase c), not "Verify Code"]

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that disabling 2FA with a valid password removes 2FA controls from the My Account page
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. Two-factor authentication is already enabled on the account.

steps.content: Click "Disable 2FA."
steps.expected: The "Disable two-factor authentication (2FA)?" modal should be displayed with a warning that 2FA will need to be set up again in future, a password field labelled "Enter your password to disable 2FA," and "Cancel" / "Disable 2FA" buttons.

steps.content: Enter the account password and click "Disable 2FA."
steps.expected: The "Disable 2FA" and "Re-generate recovery codes" buttons should no longer be displayed, and a "Set up 2FA" button should be displayed instead. [confirmed: end-state verified live; the transient confirmation toaster itself was not captured in time to confirm its exact text]

cases.rewrite_notes: Merged modal-open + happy-path into one flow (collapses 3 Suite 6 groups).

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that cancelling the Disable 2FA modal keeps 2FA enabled
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Disable two-factor authentication (2FA)?" modal is open.

steps.content: Click "Cancel."
steps.expected: The modal should close and the "Disable 2FA" / "Re-generate recovery codes" buttons should remain displayed. [confirmed live]

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that disabling 2FA with an incorrect password shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Disable two-factor authentication (2FA)?" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter an incorrect password and click "Disable 2FA."
steps.expected: "Invalid password" error message should be displayed.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that re-generating 2FA recovery codes with a valid password displays a new set of recovery codes
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. Two-factor authentication is already enabled on the account.

steps.content: Click "Re-generate recovery codes."
steps.expected: The "Re-generate your 2FA recovery codes" modal should be displayed with a warning about regenerating codes, a password field labelled "Enter your password to re-generate recovery codes," and "Cancel" / "Re-generate recovery codes" buttons (the latter disabled by default).

steps.content: Enter the account password and click "Re-generate recovery codes."
steps.expected: A "Your 2FA recovery codes" modal should be displayed with a table of 12 ten-digit recovery codes, a "Copy" button, an "I’ve securely saved my 2FA recovery codes." checkbox, and an "Okay" button disabled by default.

steps.content: Click the "Copy" button.
steps.expected: The 12 recovery codes should be copied.

steps.content: Check the checkbox and click "Okay."
steps.expected: The modal should close.

cases.rewrite_notes: Consolidated 7 fragmented Suite 6 cases into one flow.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that re-generating recovery codes with an incorrect password shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Re-generate your 2FA recovery codes" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter an incorrect password and click "Re-generate recovery codes."
steps.expected: "Invalid password" error message should be displayed.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that deleting the account requires checking a confirmation checkbox before the Delete Account button is enabled
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page.

steps.content: Click "Delete Account" in the Account deletion section.
steps.expected: The "Delete account?" modal should be displayed with an explanation that deletion is permanent, a warning that the user will need to sign up again to use CookieYes in future, an "I understand and want to delete my account including all its data." checkbox, and "Cancel" / "Delete Account" buttons (the latter disabled by default).

steps.content: Check the "I understand and want to delete my account including all its data." checkbox.
steps.expected: The checkbox should be checked and the "Delete Account" button should become enabled.

steps.content: Click "Delete Account."
steps.expected: [needs-manual-check] Not executed live — this is the only seeded QA2 test account available, and account deletion is irreversible. Steps 1-2 (modal display, checkbox enabling the button) were confirmed live and matched exactly, including that the "contact us" link points to the external https://www.cookieyes.com/support (also see Draft Case 26, skipped:external-url). This final step should be verified manually, ideally against a disposable test account.

cases.rewrite_notes: Consolidated 4 fragmented Suite 6 cases into one flow.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that the "contact us" link in the Delete account modal opens the support page
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Delete account?" modal is open.

steps.content: Click the "contact us" link.
steps.expected: The Support page should be displayed.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that the Manage Email Preferences page allows toggling notification categories
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page.

steps.content: Click "Manage email preferences" in the Email notifications section.
steps.expected: The "Manage your email preferences" page should be displayed, showing the registered email address and a note: "Changes to your preferences won't affect important account emails, such as invoices and scan reports."

steps.content: Observe the preference categories listed.
steps.expected:
Three categories should be displayed, each with its own checkbox:
- Product Updates — "Stay in the loop on new features, improvements and upcoming releases."
- Marketing — "Receive promotional offers, discounts, and exclusive deals."
- Newsletters — "Stay updated on news, tips and blogs in our monthly digest."

steps.content: Verify the "Save preferences" button state before making any changes.
steps.expected: The "Save preferences" button should be disabled by default.

steps.content: Check the "Product Updates" checkbox.
steps.expected: The checkbox should be checked and the "Save preferences" button should become enabled.

steps.content: Click "Save preferences."
steps.expected: [needs-manual-check] Not verified live — the checkbox was toggled and reverted without saving to avoid mutating the shared QA2 test account's email subscription state. Verify the save confirmation and that the preference persists on reload, ideally against a disposable test account.

steps.content: Click "Close preferences."
steps.expected: The user should be returned to the My Account page.

cases.rewrite_notes: New case — gap found live. This entire "Email notifications" section (Manage email preferences) does not exist anywhere in the Suite 6 source; it postdates the legacy suite.

cases.title: [My Account] Verify that updating the email to an address already registered to another account shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the My Account page. The "Update your email address" modal is open.

steps.content: Enter an email address that is already registered to a CookieYes account in "New email address" and "Confirm email address," and enter the correct current password.
steps.expected: The values should be accepted in all fields.

steps.content: Click "Update email."
steps.expected: "The new email has already been taken." error message should be displayed as a general form message above the Cancel/Update email buttons.

cases.rewrite_notes: New case — gap found live while testing Draft Case 3's flow using the account's own current email as input (a trivial case of "already registered"). Not covered by any Suite 6 source case.
